jokes.text: I yanked at her... I cleaned my glasses. Her shirttail was sticking out. I cleaned my glasses.
jokes.setup: Lawyer mentions Larry yanked at assistant's clothes in harassment complaint
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely unrelated explanation about cleaning his glasses
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic - completely missing the point of a serious accusation with bizarre non-sequitur. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: If she gets anything at all here, it would be a travesty. And I would, I would leave the country. I don't know where I'd go, but I would leave.
jokes.setup: Sexual harassment case against Larry
jokes.punchline: Dramatic threat to leave the country over what seems like a minor incident
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's tendency to catastrophize minor situations. Character-appropriate overreaction.

jokes.text: Millions for defense. Not one cent for tribute. That's what we said when the British were impressing our seamen.
jokes.setup: Lawyer suggests settlement for harassment case
jokes.punchline: Larry invokes obscure historical reference about British naval impressment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Larry moment - treating sexual harassment like the War of 1812. The historical accuracy makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, there! We have the best dental plan in all of Europe. Why, look at our bicorn hats and fancy brass buttons. How's that, sailors? Are you impressed by that?
jokes.setup: Larry explaining British naval impressment
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding 'impressment' as trying to literally impress sailors
jokes.explanation: Outstanding comedic moment. Larry's complete misunderstanding of historical term creates perfect absurdist comedy. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: In this office, we, uh, we try to use a holistic approach to compensation.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if he'll be charged for calling
jokes.punchline: Lawyer's pretentious way of saying 'yes, we charge for everything'
jokes.explanation: Decent lawyer pomposity joke, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: an infinitely better bathroom than to send me to a public... Oh, it's nothing special. You'll be much happier down the hall.
jokes.setup: Larry asks to use lawyer's private bathroom
jokes.punchline: Lawyer firmly redirecting him to public restroom despite Larry's pleading
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment setting up later bathroom callback. Shows social awkwardness well.

jokes.text: I'm like a murderer going in there. I completely expunge all evidence that I was ever there.
jokes.setup: Larry promising to leave bathroom exactly as he found it
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to a murderer covering up a crime
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation. Larry's murderer comparison for bathroom etiquette is perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: Motion denied, as they say in the profession.
jokes.setup: Larry's elaborate bathroom request
jokes.punchline: Lawyer using legal terminology to deny bathroom access
jokes.explanation: Nice legal wordplay that caps the bathroom sequence cleanly.

jokes.text: It's not about the money, okay? It's about having to go into your pants pocket, in a car when you're paying. Have you ever been in a car trying to get to your pocket?
jokes.setup: Parking attendant questions if Larry needs validation
jokes.punchline: Larry's elaborate explanation about the difficulty of reaching into pockets while sitting
jokes.explanation: Good Larry minutiae observation. Character-appropriate fixation on small inconveniences.

jokes.text: This is why homeless people don't make a lot of money from people in cars, 'cause nobody wants to go into their pockets.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining difficulty of reaching into pockets in cars
jokes.punchline: Connecting this to why homeless people don't get donations from drivers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational comedy that connects mundane inconvenience to larger social issue. Dark but insightful.

jokes.text: This is only for an hour. I was here for at least an hour-ten.
jokes.setup: Getting parking validation
jokes.punchline: Larry demanding compensation for the extra 10 minutes
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry pettiness, though not particularly novel.

jokes.text: I would like to test you for thyroid cancer.
jokes.setup: Doctor calling Larry
jokes.punchline: Abrupt, blunt delivery of cancer test news
jokes.explanation: Sets up the cancer storyline but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: if you do have cancer... I can't be your friend anymore. I-I'm not a cancer friend. I can't do the cancer friend.
jokes.setup: Larry sharing his potential cancer diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately declaring he can't be friends with cancer patients
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy moment. Jeff's casual cruelty is shocking and funny. 'Cancer friend' is a great coinage.

jokes.text: If you get cancer, I'll never speak to you again.
jokes.setup: Jeff's cancer friend declaration
jokes.punchline: Richard immediately agreeing and escalating
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jeff's bit, showing how quickly cruel logic spreads in the group.

jokes.text: When I found out my daddy had cancer, I never spoke to that man again. I even stopped taking his calls.
jokes.setup: Group declaring they won't be friends with cancer patients
jokes.punchline: Leon revealing he actually did this to his own father
jokes.explanation: Outstanding dark comedy escalation. Leon's matter-of-fact delivery makes it even more shocking and funny.

jokes.text: Did he die? I don't know.
jokes.setup: Leon never spoke to his cancer-stricken father again
jokes.punchline: Leon doesn't even know if his father died
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the cancer friend sequence. Leon's casual indifference is beautifully dark and economical.

jokes.text: Let's make a little deal here, okay? A no-cancer friend reciprocity pact.
jokes.setup: Group discussing not being friends with cancer patients
jokes.punchline: Larry formalizing their cruel logic into an official pact
jokes.explanation: Good Larry response - trying to systematize the group's cruelty. 'Reciprocity pact' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: They're all over. It's an epidemic. I'm gonna end the wobbly table epidemic, in my coffee house.
jokes.setup: Larry noticing another wobbly table
jokes.punchline: Declaring war on wobbly tables with grandiose language
jokes.explanation: Good Larry fixation on minor annoyances. Sets up the spite store subplot nicely.

jokes.text: I think I'll do a little, uh, a little side-sitting.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual introduction of 'side-sitting' as a concept
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's title concept. Larry treating it like a normal thing is funny.

jokes.text: Well, it's, uh, very intimate. This is too far to talk to somebody. It feels a little close.
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting side-sitting
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's discomfort with the intimacy, then contradicting herself
jokes.explanation: Sets up side-sitting as awkward but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: It's my mailman. His name's Lionel... Something about that guy gives me the creeps.
jokes.setup: Cheryl introducing mailman Lionel
jokes.punchline: Immediately saying he gives her the creeps after friendly introduction
jokes.explanation: Good setup for mailman subplot. The immediate contradiction after polite introduction works.

jokes.text: Can I introduce you to my friend, the elephant?
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl having awkward conversation
jokes.punchline: Cheryl referring to the obvious topic (their relationship) as 'the elephant'
jokes.explanation: Decent metaphor for addressing their relationship issues directly.

jokes.text: Hypothetically, suppose she told me I had... two years. Would that alter your thinking about us at all?
jokes.setup: Cheryl rejecting getting back together
jokes.punchline: Larry trying to use hypothetical terminal diagnosis as relationship leverage
jokes.explanation: Great dark Larry moment - trying to manipulate ex-wife with fake terminal diagnosis is perfectly awful.

jokes.text: I can get a nurse and complain to the nurse and leave you out of that part.
jokes.setup: Larry pitching two-year relationship with terminal diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Promising to complain to a nurse instead of Cheryl
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Larry's self-awareness about his complaining while still being manipulative.

jokes.text: Would you mind if I brought the nurse along?
jokes.setup: Larry suggesting travel during his hypothetical final two years
jokes.punchline: Asking if he can bring his nurse on romantic trips
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of the manipulative dying man fantasy.

jokes.text: I don't give a fuck.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking what Larry's going to get to eat
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete indifference after failed reconciliation attempt
jokes.explanation: Good deflated response showing Larry's disappointment through indifference.

jokes.text: You think rich beats old and bald? You could have mutton chops and wear a cartoon tie of Felix the Cat... and wear an Abe Lincoln hat. You'll be fine.
jokes.setup: Larry worried about being old and bald for dating
jokes.punchline: Jeff's ridiculous examples of what rich men can get away with
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist imagery. The specific details (Felix the Cat tie, Abe Lincoln hat) make it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: The other night at poker, that invitation, it, it didn't get lost. You made that whole thing up. Blame it on the mailman.
jokes.setup: Earlier Larry claimed invitation got lost in mail
jokes.punchline: Larry admitting he lied and blamed the mailman
jokes.explanation: Good callback that sets up the mailman confrontation later. Shows Larry's casual dishonesty.

jokes.text: You've never gotten me a birthday gift. I did this year!
jokes.setup: Susie surprised by getting a gift from Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive pride about finally giving a gift
jokes.explanation: Sets up the painting reveal. Larry's rare moment of pride in gift-giving.

jokes.text: From you, of all people, 'cause you're not a giver.
jokes.setup: Susie loving Larry's gift
jokes.punchline: Susie accurately assessing Larry's character while praising him
jokes.explanation: Good character insight delivered as backhanded compliment.

jokes.text: All is forgiven. Everything you've ever done, every horrible thing you've done to me is forgiven for this.
jokes.setup: Susie loving the painting gift
jokes.punchline: Dramatic forgiveness for all of Larry's past wrongs
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the painting destruction - makes it even more devastating when Larry ruins it.

jokes.text: I thought I told you a camera, a fucking camera. This is mahogany cream. You fucked me.
jokes.setup: Larry discovering the gift mix-up
jokes.punchline: Larry's rage at getting wrong item despite loving reaction
jokes.explanation: Good Larry moment - can't enjoy success because it wasn't what he intended.

jokes.text: So, I'm the mail. Yes. In this particular case... I'm the guy that is delivering the mail, so that is a stain on an otherwise perfect record.
jokes.setup: Larry blamed lost invitation on mail
jokes.punchline: Mailman taking personal offense at his professional reputation
jokes.explanation: Good callback with mailman's professional pride. 'I'm the mail' is a nice line.

jokes.text: If you're gonna confront somebody and ask them to do something like this, it's better to do it with pants on.
jokes.setup: Mailman confronting Larry in uniform shorts
jokes.punchline: Larry's advice about confrontational attire
jokes.explanation: Good Larry observation about authority and clothing. Typical Larry deflection.

jokes.text: How many pairs? What does that have to do with this?
jokes.setup: Larry asking about postal service sock allocation
jokes.punchline: Mailman's confusion at Larry's random tangent
jokes.explanation: Good Larry deflection technique - getting distracted by irrelevant details during confrontation.

jokes.text: Ah! Ha-ha-ha-ha! Oh, thank you. Thank you! Ah! Negative! Negative!
jokes.setup: Doctor calling with test results
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-the-top celebration of negative cancer test
jokes.explanation: Good physical celebration moment, sets up friends not checking on him later.

jokes.text: it's been two days since I found out, and not one of my friends has inquired as to how the test turned out, not one.
jokes.setup: Larry telling lawyer about good test results
jokes.punchline: Larry's complaint about friends not following up
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier cancer friend discussion. Shows Larry's need for attention even with good news.

jokes.text: Did you use my bathroom? What? No. Absolutely not.
jokes.setup: Larry in Swindell's office
jokes.punchline: Swindell confronting Larry about bathroom use he earlier denied
jokes.explanation: Good callback creating tension. Sets up Larry potentially being caught in lie.

jokes.text: Beautiful view here. How long did it take you to take it for granted?
jokes.setup: Larry with parking attendant Rita
jokes.punchline: Larry's cynical take on appreciating beautiful things
jokes.explanation: Good Larry observation about human nature and habituation to beauty.

jokes.text: It would take me about a day, maybe less, maybe a half an hour.
jokes.setup: Larry asking how long to take view for granted
jokes.punchline: Larry's escalating estimate of his own cynicism
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's self-awareness about his own cynical nature.

jokes.text: Oh, dear God, please let me meet an old bald man, who will take me toilet-hopping, from toilet to toilet, throughout the town. Please, God. Please make that happen.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Rita on toilet-hopping date
jokes.punchline: Larry sarcastically voicing what he imagines is Rita's dream scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating moment. Larry's awareness of how unappealing he is makes it funnier. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Somebody told me you were side-sitting Cheryl. The mailman.
jokes.setup: Leon confronting Larry about side-sitting
jokes.punchline: Revealing the mailman is reporting on Larry's activities
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting mailman confrontation to side-sitting revelation.

jokes.text: If you ever see me sitting, you'll see me side-sitting.
jokes.setup: Leon accusing Larry of only side-sitting Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Larry claiming he side-sits with everyone
jokes.explanation: Good Larry defense - making his weird behavior seem universal rather than specific.

jokes.text: Why couldn't you get her a camera?! I should have given her the camera. Next time get her a camera! Believe me, there won't be a next time!
jokes.setup: Jeff angry about painting disaster
jokes.punchline: Circular argument about camera gift ending with Larry swearing off gifts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of gift-giving frustration, callbacks to earlier camera discussion.

jokes.text: I will never give anyone a gift again. What do you think about that? I think it's the best idea you've had all day! My birthday's coming up in a month! What are you gettin' me?! Nothin'!
jokes.setup: Larry swearing off gift-giving
jokes.punchline: Jeff immediately asking what he's getting for his birthday after agreeing it's a good idea
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing how quickly people contradict themselves when it affects them personally.

jokes.text: You don't play, and you borrowed a piano?
jokes.setup: Rita mentioning she borrowed a piano but doesn't play
jokes.punchline: Larry's confusion at the logic of borrowing an instrument you can't use
jokes.explanation: Decent Larry logic questioning, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: I'd like to begin by taking my right hand and place it on your right shoulder. Would that be acceptable?
jokes.setup: Larry's formal consent process
jokes.punchline: Treating basic touch like a contract negotiation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the consent concept, making simple touch absurdly formal.

jokes.text: With your permission, of course, I would like to take a video in order to document the evening. Just in case there are any discrepancies of the he-said, she-said variety.
jokes.setup: Larry's elaborate consent process
jokes.punchline: Larry wanting to videotape their encounter for legal protection
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that pushes the consent anxiety to absurd extreme. Perfectly captures modern paranoia.

jokes.text: I'm going to place it on your inner thigh, and slowly inch my way up, in a crab-like fashion
jokes.setup: Larry describing his next move on camera
jokes.punchline: 'Crab-like fashion' as description for romantic touching
jokes.explanation: Outstanding absurdist description. 'Crab-like fashion' is hilariously unromantic and highly quotable.

jokes.text: It seems we've reached a bit of an impasse. End session.
jokes.setup: Rita rejecting Larry's advances
jokes.punchline: Larry formally ending the 'session' like a business meeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect capper to the consent sequence. Treating romantic failure like a failed negotiation.

jokes.text: Three days now. I've gotten the results back from my cancer test. Not one of you bothered to call me and ask me if I got the results back.
jokes.setup: Larry at dinner party with friends
jokes.punchline: Larry's public complaint about friends not checking on his test results
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier setup. Larry's neediness for attention even with good news is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: What kind of friends are you? Seriously! What kind of friends are you? You can all go fuck yourself!
jokes.setup: Larry's anger at friends for not calling
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic exit that presumably destroys the painting
jokes.explanation: Good setup for painting destruction. Larry's dramatic exit timing is perfect for maximum damage.

jokes.text: You ruined my painting! You destroyed it!
jokes.setup: Larry's dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Larry destroyed the painting Susie loved
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to earlier setup. The destruction of the thing that made Susie forgive Larry is perfect irony.

jokes.text: I think this whole thing can be a blessing in disguise. I've never been entirely happy with this painting. He didn't really capture the luster of your skin.
jokes.setup: Larry destroying beloved painting
jokes.punchline: Larry trying to spin disaster as improvement opportunity with fake art criticism
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry damage control. Pretending to have sophisticated art opinions while covering up disaster is perfect.

jokes.text: You can't side-sit with me. Oh, my God. Let go! You don't get to grab me, at a banquette or side-sit!
jokes.setup: Larry trying to side-sit with his accuser Alice
jokes.punchline: Alice's horror at Larry violating her space during harassment meeting
jokes.explanation: Great callback that makes side-sitting explicitly part of Larry's harassment pattern. Perfect escalation.

jokes.text: Did you or did you not use my bathroom?!
jokes.setup: Deposition scene with bathroom question recurring
jokes.punchline: Swindell's dramatic courtroom intensity over bathroom usage
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that elevates petty bathroom issue to courtroom drama intensity.

jokes.text: There's no other way to eat an apple but blithely. That's how apples are eaten.
jokes.setup: Leon criticizing Larry for blithely eating apple during serious situation
jokes.punchline: Larry's insistence that blithe is the only proper apple-eating method
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic about food consumption etiquette. Perfect absurdist reasoning about apple-eating methodology.

jokes.text: You are like a president in the 18th century on the back of a train saying, 'And there will always be toast with eggs.'
jokes.setup: Leon complaining about serving eggs without toast
jokes.punchline: Larry's elaborate historical analogy for Leon's breakfast proclamation
jokes.explanation: Outstanding absurdist analogy. The specific imagery of 18th century president on train making breakfast proclamations is brilliant and highly quotable.

jokes.text: I think you can introduce me sort of as a renaissance man. A guy who speaks six languages. You wanna impress people with lies?
jokes.setup: Larry wanting impressive introduction
jokes.punchline: Leon's blunt assessment of Larry's fake credentials plan
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's vanity and Leon's cutting directness.
